library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: توسعات نصية أو قيم توليدية يطبقها المعالج المسبق قبل الترجمة.

identity.name: SDL_HINT_EVENT_LOGGING
identity.titleArabic:
A variable controlling verbosity of the logging of SDL events pushed
onto the internal queue

summary.actualTranslationArabic:
A variable controlling verbosity of the logging of SDL events pushed
onto the internal queue
summary.actualMeaningArabic: يمثل هذا الماكرو ماكرو SDL HINT الحدث LOGGING، أي توسعاً نصياً أو قيمة توليدية تؤثر في الكود قبل الترجمة.
summary.carriedValueArabic: الماكرو يوسع إلى #define SDL_HINT_EVENT_LOGGING "SDL_EVENT_LOGGING" أو إلى تعبير مشتق منه، والقيمة الحقيقية هنا هي المعنى الذي يضيفه هذا التوسع إلى الكود.
summary.sourceDescription:
A variable controlling verbosity of the logging of SDL events pushed
onto the internal queue.

remarks:
The variable can be set to the following values, from least to most
verbose:
"1": Log most events (other than the really spammy ones).
"2": Include mouse and finger motion events.

This is generally meant to be used to debug SDL itself, but can be
useful for application developers that need better visibility into what
is going on in the event queue. Logged events are sent through SDL_Log (), which means by default they appear on
stdout on most platforms or maybe OutputDebugString() on Windows, and
can be funneled by the app with SDL_SetLogOutputFunction (),
etc.
This hint can be set anytime.
